title: MigrationContext::ledger_path is in MigrationContext but read by load() while migrations write via mark()
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/migrations/mod.rs:111-114, 126-168
## Violation
The ledger path is owned by MigrationContext but the lifecycle is owned by run_all:

```rust
pub fn ledger_path(&self) -> PathBuf {
    self.openclaudia_data.join("migrations.json")
}

pub fn run_all(ctx: &MigrationContext) -> Vec<MigrationReport> {
    let mut ledger = CompletionLedger::load(&ctx.ledger_path());
    ...
    if let Err(err) = ledger.save(&ctx.ledger_path()) {  // bottom of fn
        tracing::warn!(error = %err, "failed to persist migration ledger");
    }
    out
}
```

The MigrationContext exposes ledger_path but nothing else interacts with the ledger through the context — the ledger itself is a local variable in run_all. This breaks the "context object" abstraction: callers cannot inspect or mock the ledger via MigrationContext for testing.

More importantly, individual Migration impls receive &MigrationContext but the ledger inside it is invisible to them. Once-only migrations have no way to know whether they're being re-run (a debugging affordance), nor can they participate in their own once-only logic (e.g., a migration that records additional metadata alongside its completion mark).

## Mandated refactor
1. Move the ledger ownership to MigrationContext as an interior-mutable field (RefCell<CompletionLedger>) so individual migrations can read/write it through the context.
2. Or, hide the ledger entirely as a private detail of run_all and remove ledger_path from the public MigrationContext API — the path leakage suggests an incomplete abstraction.

Pick one extreme; the current half-encapsulation forces callers to know about both the path and the loader/saver but gives them no leverage.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
